moveAnnotations.annotation: We're playing the Kádas Opening, an unusual first move that immediately stakes a claim on the kingside by advancing the rook pawn. Unlike the central moves (e4, d4) or solid flanchetto preparation (b3, g3), h4 is unconventional—it doesn't fight for the center immediately and weakens our kingside slightly. However, this move has a specific idea: we're preparing g4 to launch a kingside pawn storm, or sometimes we're simply trying to provoke Black into an immediate counterattack in the center where we can respond actively. The move is rare at top levels because it allows Black to seize space with moves like e5 or d5, but it can catch unprepared opponents off-guard and lead to interesting, off-beat positions where creativity matters more than theory.
